name: Chicken Egg Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole large chicken breast, skinned, halved and boned
16 oz Can bean sprouts, drained (or fresh, rinsed and Patted dry)
1/2 cup Chopped celery in small pieces
8 Egg roll skins or packaged egg roll skins
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chicken by cutting it into bite-sized pieces.
2. In a bowl, whisk together the cooking oil, minced garlic, soy sauce, cornstarch, and five spice powder until well combined.
3. Add the chopped bean sprouts and celery to the mixture. Stir to coat evenly.
4. Add the chicken pieces to the mixture and toss gently to ensure they are coated with the marinade.
5. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
6. Place the egg roll skins on the prepared baking sheet, skin side up.
7. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of the chicken mixture into each egg roll skin.
8. Roll the egg rolls tightly, sealing the open end by folding it over and pressing to create an airtight seal.
9. Heat the cooking oil in a deep skillet or pot to about 350°F (175°C).
10. Once the oil is hot, carefully place the egg rolls into the pan one at a time, ensuring they don't touch each other.
11. Deep-fry the egg rolls until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
12. Remove the fried egg rolls from the oil using a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
13. Turn off the heat under the oil once you have finished frying all the egg rolls.
14. Allow the egg rolls to cool for a few minutes before serving.
